req.title: Joint account operations
req.statement: A joint-account holder shall perform transactions; the secondary holder shall not be able to modify ownership.
error_pathways: 403 when secondary holder attempts ownership change

tests.name: naive_case
tests.executable:
# Pytest Tests for Joint Account Holder Requirements

```python
"""
Tests for joint account holder transaction and ownership requirements.

Requirement: A joint-account holder shall perform transactions;
             the secondary holder shall not be able to modify ownership.
"""

import pytest
from decimal import Decimal
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch
from datetime import datetime


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Domain models / exceptions (assumed application interfaces)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
from banking.accounts import JointAccount, AccountOwnership
from banking.transactions import (
    DepositTransaction,
    WithdrawalTransaction,
    TransferTransaction,
)
from banking.exceptions import (
    InsufficientFundsError,
    UnauthorizedOwnershipModificationError,
    InvalidAccountHolderError,
    PermissionDeniedError,
)
from banking.roles import AccountRole


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Constants
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
PRIMARY_HOLDER_ID = "user-primary-001"
SECONDARY_HOLDER_ID = "user-secondary-002"
THIRD_PARTY_ID = "user-third-003"

INITIAL_BALANCE = Decimal("1000.00")
SMALL_AMOUNT = Decimal("50.00")
LARGE_AMOUNT = Decimal("10000.00")


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Fixtures
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@pytest.fixture
def primary_holder():
    holder = MagicMock()
    holder.user_id = PRIMARY_HOLDER_ID
    holder.role = AccountRole.PRIMARY
    holder.is_active = True
    return holder


@pytest.fixture
def secondary_holder():
    holder = MagicMock()
    holder.user_id = SECONDARY_HOLDER_ID
    holder.role = AccountRole.SECONDARY
    holder.is_active = True
    return holder


@pytest.fixture
def third_party_user():
    user = MagicMock()
    user.user_id = THIRD_PARTY_ID
    user.role = None
    user.is_active = True
    return user


@pytest.fixture
def joint_account(primary_holder, secondary_holder):
    account = JointAccount(
        account_id="acct-joint-001",
        primary_holder=primary_holder,
        secondary_holder=secondary_holder,
        balance=INITIAL_BALANCE,
        currency="USD",
    )
    return account


@pytest.fixture
def zero_balance_joint_account(primary_holder, secondary_holder):
    return JointAccount(
        account_id="acct-joint-zero-002",
        primary_holder=primary_holder,
        secondary_holder=secondary_holder,
        balance=Decimal("0.00"),
        currency="USD",
    )


@pytest.fixture
def destination_account():
    account = MagicMock()
    account.account_id = "acct-dest-999"
    account.currency = "USD"
    account.is_active = True
    return account


# ===========================================================================
# 1. SECONDARY HOLDER — TRANSACTION PERMISSIONS
# ===========================================================================

class TestSecondaryHolderCanDeposit:
    """Secondary holder must be able to perform deposit transactions."""

    def test_secondary_holder_deposit_succeeds(self, joint_account, secondary_holder):
        """Secondary holder can deposit funds into the joint account."""
        initial_balance = joint_account.balance
        txn = DepositTransaction(
            account=joint_account,
            initiated_by=secondary_holder,
            amount=SMALL_AMOUNT,
        )

        result = txn.execute()

        assert result.success is True
        assert joint_account.balance == initial_balance + SMALL_AMOUNT

    def test_secondary_holder_deposit_returns_transaction_record(
        self, joint_account, secondary_holder
    ):
        """Deposit by secondary holder produces a valid transaction record."""
        txn = DepositTransaction(
            account=joint_account,
            initiated_by=secondary_holder,
            amount=SMALL_AMOUNT,
        )

        result = txn.execute()

        assert result.transaction_id is not None
        assert result.amount == SMALL_AMOUNT
        assert result.initiated_by == SECONDARY_HOLDER_ID
        assert isinstance(result.timestamp, datetime)

    def test_secondary_holder_deposit_zero_amount_raises(
        self, joint_account, secondary_holder
    ):
        """Depositing zero is invalid regardless of who initiates it."""
        with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="amount must be greater than zero"):
            DepositTransaction(
                account=joint_account,
                initiated_by=secondary_holder,
                amount=Decimal("0.00"),
            )

    def test_secondary_holder_deposit_negative_amount_raises(
        self, joint_account, secondary_holder
    ):
        """Negative deposit amounts are rejected."""
        with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="amount must be greater than zero"):
            DepositTransaction(
                account=joint_account,
                initiated_by=secondary_holder,
                amount=Decimal("-100.00"),
            )


class TestSecondaryHolderCanWithdraw:
    """Secondary holder must be able to perform withdrawal transactions."""

    def test_secondary_holder_withdrawal_succeeds(self, joint_account, secondary_holder):
        """Secondary holder can withdraw available funds."""
        initial_balance = joint_account.balance
        txn = WithdrawalTransaction(
            account=joint_account,
            initiated_by=secondary_holder,
            amount=SMALL_AMOUNT,
        )

        result = txn.execute()

        assert result.success is True
        assert joint_account.balance == initial_balance - SMALL_AMOUNT

    def test_secondary_holder_withdrawal_insufficient_funds_raises(
        self, zero_balance_joint_account, secondary_holder
    ):
        """Withdrawal beyond available balance raises InsufficientFundsError."""
        txn = WithdrawalTransaction(
            account=zero_balance_joint_account,
            initiated_by=secondary_holder,
            amount=SMALL_AMOUNT,
        )

        with pytest.raises(InsufficientFundsError):
            txn.execute()

    def test_secondary_holder_withdrawal_exact_balance_succeeds(
        self, joint_account, secondary_holder
    ):
        """Secondary holder can withdraw the entire available balance."""
        txn = WithdrawalTransaction(
            account=joint_account,
            initiated_by=secondary_holder,
            amount=joint_account.balance,
        )

        result = txn.execute()

        assert result.success is True
        assert joint_account.balance == Decimal("0.00")

    def test_secondary_holder_withdrawal_updates_balance(
        self, joint_account, secondary_holder
    ):
        """Account balance is correctly decremented after withdrawal."""
        amount = Decimal("200.00")
        expected_balance = joint_account.balance - amount
        txn = WithdrawalTransaction(
            account=joint_account,
            initiated_by=secondary_holder,
            amount=amount,
        )

        txn.execute()

        assert joint_account.balance == expected_balance


class TestSecondaryHolderCanTransfer:
    """Secondary holder must be able to initiate transfer transactions."""

    def test_secondary_holder_transfer_succeeds(
        self, joint_account, secondary_holder, destination_account
    ):
        """Secondary holder can transfer funds to another account."""
        initial_balance = joint_account.balance
        txn = TransferTransaction(
            source_account=joint_account,
            destination_account=destination_account,
            initiated_by=secondary_holder,
            amount=SMALL_AMOUNT,
        )

        result = txn.execute()

        assert result.success is True
        assert joint_account.balance == initial_balance - SMALL_AMOUNT

    def test_secondary_holder_transfer_insufficient_funds_raises(
        self, zero_balance_joint_account, secondary_holder, destination_account
    ):
        """Transfer beyond balance raises InsufficientFundsError."""
        txn = TransferTransaction(
            source_account=zero_balance_joint_account,
            destination_account=destination_account,
            initiated_by=secondary_holder,
            amount=SMALL_AMOUNT,
        )

        with pytest.raises(InsufficientFundsError):
            txn.execute()

    def test_secondary_holder_transfer_to_same_account_raises(
        self, joint_account, secondary_holder
    ):
        """Transfer to the same account is not allowed."""
        with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="cannot transfer to the same account"):
            TransferTransaction(
                source_account=joint_account,
                destination_account=joint_account,
                initiated_by=secondary_holder,
                amount=SMALL_AMOUNT,
            )


# ===========================================================================
# 2. PRIMARY HOLDER — TRANSACTION PERMISSIONS (baseline / regression)
# ===========================================================================

class TestPrimaryHolderCanPerformTransactions:
    """Primary holder retains full transaction capabilities."""

    def test_primary_holder_deposit_succeeds(self, joint_account, primary_holder):
        txn = DepositTransaction(
            account=joint_account,
            initiated_by=primary_holder,
            amount=SMALL_AMOUNT,
        )
        result = txn.execute()
        assert result.success is True

    def test_primary_holder_withdrawal_succeeds(self, joint_account, primary_holder):
        txn = WithdrawalTransaction(
            account=joint_account,
            initiated_by=primary_holder,
            amount=SMALL_AMOUNT,
        )
        result = txn.execute()
        assert result.success is True

    def test_primary_holder_transfer_succeeds(
        self, joint_account, primary_holder, destination_account
    ):
        txn = TransferTransaction(
            source_account=joint_account,
            destination_account=destination_account,
            initiated_by=primary_holder,
            amount=SMALL_AMOUNT,
        )
        result = txn.execute()
        assert result.success is True


# ===========================================================================
# 3. SECONDARY HOLDER — OWNERSHIP MODIFICATION RESTRICTIONS
# ===========================================================================

class TestSecondaryHolderCannotModifyOwnership:
    """
    Core requirement: the secondary holder must NOT be able to
    modify account ownership in any form.
    """

    def test_secondary_holder_cannot_add_new_owner(
        self, joint_account, secondary_holder, third_party_user
    ):
        """Adding a new account holder is forbidden for the secondary holder."""
        with pytest.raises(UnauthorizedOwnershipModificationError):
            joint_account.add_owner(
                requester=secondary_holder,
                new_owner=third_party_user,
            )

    def test_secondary_holder_cannot_remove_primary_holder(
        self, joint_account, secondary_holder, primary_holder
    ):
        """Secondary holder cannot remove the primary holder."""
        with pytest.raises(UnauthorizedOwnershipModificationError):
            joint_account.remove_owner(
                requester=secondary_holder,
                target_owner=primary_holder,
            )

    def test_secondary_holder_cannot_remove_themselves(
        self, joint_account, secondary_holder
    ):
        """Secondary holder cannot remove themselves from the account."""
        with pytest.raises(UnauthorizedOwnershipModificationError):
            joint_account.remove_owner(
                requester=secondary_holder,
                target_owner=secondary_holder,
            )

    def test_secondary_holder_cannot_transfer_primary_role(
        self, joint_account, secondary_holder, third_party_user
    ):
        """Secondary holder cannot elevate another user to primary holder."""
        with pytest.raises(UnauthorizedOwnershipModificationError):
            joint_account.change_primary_holder(
                requester=secondary_holder,
                new_primary=third_party_user,
            )

    def test_secondary_holder_cannot_promote_themselves_to_primary(
        self, joint_account, secondary_holder
    ):
        """Secondary holder cannot promote themselves to primary."""
        with pytest.raises(UnauthorizedOwnershipModificationError):
            joint_account.change_primary_holder(
                requester=secondary_holder,
                new_primary=secondary_holder,
            )

    def test_secondary_holder_cannot_change_ownership_percentage(
        self, joint_account, secondary_holder
    ):
        """Secondary holder cannot alter ownership share/percentage."""
        with pytest.raises(UnauthorizedOwnershipModificationError):
            joint_account.update_ownership_percentage(
                requester=secondary_holder,
                holder_id=SECONDARY_HOLDER_ID,
                new_percentage=Decimal("75.00"),
            )

    def test_secondary_holder_ownership_modification_does_not_alter_state(
        self, joint_account, secondary_holder, third_party_user
    ):
        """Account ownership remains unchanged after a rejected modification attempt."""
        owners_before = joint_account.get_owner_ids()

        with pytest.raises(UnauthorizedOwnershipModificationError):
            joint_account.add_owner(
                requester=secondary_holder,
                new_owner=third_party_user,
            )

        owners_after = joint_account.get_owner_ids()
        assert owners_before == owners_after

    def test_secondary_holder_ownership_error_contains_holder_id(
        self, joint_account, secondary_holder, third_party_user
    ):
        """Exception message identifies the unauthorized requester."""
        with pytest.raises(
            UnauthorizedOwnershipModificationError, match=SECONDARY_HOLDER_ID
        ):
            joint_account.add_owner(
                requester=secondary_holder,
                new_owner=third_party_user,
            )

    @pytest.mark.parametrize(
        "ownership_method, kwargs_builder",
        [
            (
                "add_owner",
                lambda third_party: {"new_owner": third_party},
            ),
            (
                "change_primary_holder",
                lambda third_party: {"new_primary": third_party},
            ),
        ],
    )
    def test_secondary_holder_all_ownership_mutations_are_blocked(
        self,
        joint_account,
        secondary_holder,
        third_party_user,
        ownership_method,
        kwargs_builder,
    ):
        """Parametrized check: every ownership mutation is blocked."""
        method = getattr(joint_account, ownership_method)
        kwargs = kwargs_builder(third_party_user)

        with pytest.raises(UnauthorizedOwnershipModificationError):
            method(requester=secondary_holder, **kwargs)


# ===========================================================================
# 4. PRIMARY HOLDER — OWNERSHIP MODIFICATION PERMISSIONS
# ===========================================================================

class TestPrimaryHolderCanModifyOwnership:
    """Primary holder retains exclusive rights to modify ownership."""

    def test_primary_holder_can_add_new_owner(
        self, joint_account, primary_holder, third_party_user
    ):
        """Primary holder successfully adds a new owner."""
        joint_account.add_owner(
            requester=primary_holder,
            new_owner=third_party_user,
        )

        assert THIRD_PARTY_ID in joint_account.get_owner_ids()

    def test_primary_holder_can_remove_secondary_holder(
        self, joint_account, primary_holder, secondary_holder
    ):
        """Primary holder can remove the secondary holder."""
        joint_account.remove_owner(
            requester=primary_holder,
            target_owner=secondary_holder,
        )

        assert SECONDARY_HOLDER_ID not in joint_account.get_owner_ids()

    def test_primary_holder_can_change_primary_holder(
        self, joint_account, primary_holder, third_party_user
    ):
        """Primary holder can transfer primary role to another user."""
        joint_account.change_primary_holder(
            requester=primary_holder,
            new_primary=third_party_user,
        )

        assert joint_account.primary_holder.user_id == THIRD_PARTY_ID


# ===========================================================================
# 5. UNAUTHORIZED / THIRD-PARTY ACCESS
# ===========================================================================

class TestThirdPartyAccessIsDenied:
    """Non-account holders must not perform any account operations."""

    def test_third_party_cannot_deposit(self, joint_account, third_party_user):
        with pytest.raises((InvalidAccountHolderError, PermissionDeniedError)):
            txn = DepositTransaction(
                account=joint_account,
                initiated_by=third_party_user,
                amount=SMALL_AMOUNT,
            )
            txn.execute()

    def test_third_party_cannot_withdraw(self, joint_account, third_party_user):
        with pytest.raises((InvalidAccountHolderError, PermissionDeniedError)):
            txn = WithdrawalTransaction(
                account=joint_account,
                initiated_by=third_party_user,
                amount=SMALL_AMOUNT,
            )
            txn.execute()

    def test_third_party_cannot_modify_ownership(
        self, joint_account, third_party_user
    ):
        with pytest.raises((InvalidAccountHolderError, PermissionDeniedError)):
            joint_account.add_owner(
                requester=third_party_user,
                new_owner=MagicMock(user_id="user-new-999"),
            )


# ===========================================================================
# 6. AUDIT TRAIL
# ===========================================================================

class TestTransactionAuditTrail:
    """Every transaction must produce an auditable record."""

    def test_secondary_holder_transaction_is_logged(
        self, joint_account, secondary_holder
    ):
        """Successful transaction by secondary holder appears in audit log."""
        txn = DepositTransaction(
            account=joint_account,
            initiated_by=secondary_holder,
            amount=SMALL_AMOUNT,
        )
        txn.execute()

        logs = joint_account.get_transaction_history()
        assert any(
            entry.initiated_by == SECONDARY_HOLDER_ID for entry in logs
        )

    def test_blocked_ownership_change_is_logged(
        self, joint_account, secondary_holder, third_party_user
    ):
        """Rejected ownership change attempt is captured in the security log."""
        with pytest.raises(UnauthorizedOwnershipModificationError):
            joint_account.add_owner(
                requester=secondary_holder,
                new_owner=third_party_user,
            )

        security_logs = joint_account.get_security_event_log()
        assert any(
            event.event_type == "UNAUTHORIZED_OWNERSHIP_MODIFICATION"
            and event.actor_id == SECONDARY_HOLDER_ID
            for event in security_logs
        )
```

---

## Design Notes

| Area | Decision |
|---|---|
| **Fixtures** | Shared `joint_account`, `primary_holder`, `secondary_holder` fixtures isolate test data |
| **Parametrize** | Used for ownership mutation methods to avoid repetition |
| **Exception specificity** | Domain-specific exceptions (`UnauthorizedOwnershipModificationError`) are preferred over generic ones |
| **State invariance** | Tests explicitly assert no side-effects occur after a rejected call |
| **Third-party tests** | Extra guard tests ensure non-holders are also blocked |
| **Audit log tests** | Both successful transactions and rejections should be traceable |
| **Decimal** | Monetary values use `Decimal` to avoid floating-point errors |

> **Note:** The imports at the top reference the application modules you need to implement (`banking.accounts`, `banking.transactions`, etc.). Adjust the import paths to match your actual project structure.

Tests for joint account holder transaction and ownership requirements.

Requirement: A joint-account holder shall perform transactions;
             the secondary holder shall not be able to modify ownership.

from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch
from datetime import datetime

# Domain models / exceptions (assumed application interfaces)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
from banking.accounts import JointAccount, AccountOwnership

    DepositTransaction,
    WithdrawalTransaction,
    TransferTransaction,

    InsufficientFundsError,
    UnauthorizedOwnershipModificationError,
    InvalidAccountHolderError,
    PermissionDeniedError,

SMALL_AMOUNT = Decimal("50.00")
LARGE_AMOUNT = Decimal("10000.00")

# Fixtures
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

    holder = MagicMock()

    return holder

def secondary_holder():
    holder = MagicMock()
    holder.user_id = SECONDARY_HOLDER_ID
    holder.role = AccountRole.SECONDARY
    holder.is_active = True
    return holder


@pytest.fixture
def third_party_user():
    user = MagicMock()
    user.user_id = THIRD_PARTY_ID
    user.role = None
    user.is_active = True
    return user


@pytest.fixture
def joint_account(primary_holder, secondary_holder):
    account = JointAccount(
        account_id="acct-joint-001",
        primary_holder=primary_holder,
        secondary_holder=secondary_holder,
        balance=INITIAL_BALANCE,
        currency="USD",
    )
    return account


@pytest.fixture
def zero_balance_joint_account(primary_holder, secondary_holder):
    return JointAccount(
        account_id="acct-joint-zero-002",
        primary_holder=primary_holder,
        secondary_holder=secondary_holder,
        balance=Decimal("0.00"),
        currency="USD",
    )


@pytest.fixture
def destination_account():
    account = MagicMock()
    account.account_id = "acct-dest-999"
    account.currency = "USD"
    account.is_active = True
    return account


# ===========================================================================
# 1. SECONDARY HOLDER — TRANSACTION PERMISSIONS
# ===========================================================================

class TestSecondaryHolderCanDeposit:
    """Secondary holder must be able to perform deposit transactions."""

    def test_secondary_holder_deposit_succeeds(self, joint_account, secondary_holder):
        """Secondary holder can deposit funds into the joint account."""
        initial_balance = joint_account.balance

            account=joint_account,
            initiated_by=secondary_holder,
            amount=SMALL_AMOUNT,
        )

        result = txn.execute()

        assert result.success is True
        assert joint_account.balance == initial_balance + SMALL_AMOUNT

    def test_secondary_holder_deposit_returns_transaction_record(
        self, joint_account, secondary_holder
    ):
        """Deposit by secondary holder produces a valid transaction record."""
        txn = DepositTransaction(
            account=joint_account,
            initiated_by=sec
